title: SoA Table Corrections Schema
description: Human corrections to a raw soa-table-extraction. The raw extraction is immutable; corrections here are applied to produce the verified extraction that feeds resolution. Each correction is source-linked and self-describing, so the corrections corpus doubles as a provenance record and a feedback dataset for extraction-prompt refinement.

target_extraction.description: Filename of the raw extraction JSON this sidecar corrects.

id.description: Correction identifier, unique within this file (e.g. 'corr-001').

target.description: Which extraction array this correction acts on.

op.description: add=append 'set' as a new entry; set=update the single entry matching 'match'; remove=drop entries matching 'match'.

match.description: Key fields locating the target entry (e.g. table_number/row_position/column_position). Required for 'set' and 'remove'; omit for 'add'.

set.description: Field values to write. For 'add', the full new entry; for 'set', the fields to update. Omit for 'remove'.

reason.description: Human explanation of why this correction was made.

source_ref.description: Where in the source document this correction is grounded, for audit back to source.
